page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data positions this instance in the average performance tier across most compute and memory workloads. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8). In synthetic testing, the instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1728.0 and a multi-core score of 6628.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 544.86 GB/sec for cached read-only small blocks, dropping to 52.52 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Database operations via Redis SET reached 8.86 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput was measured at 13.01 GB/sec.

The c3-highcpu-8 exhibits specific performance tradeoffs, particularly in memory-intensive and large-scale AI workloads. While prompt processing for a small 135M parameter LLM model reached 726.76 tokens per second, text generation on a medium 7B model dropped to a weak 7.72 tokens per second, and 70B models are unsupported due to memory constraints. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints, such as software compilation, web serving, and lightweight vector processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. This configuration provides a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per vCPU and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6628.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 8.86 million operations per second. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which assist in vector and matrix operations. However, memory constraints limit its utility for larger workloads; text generation on a 7B LLM model is weak at 7.72 tokens per second. The instance is best suited for compute-intensive workloads with moderate memory requirements, such as software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, offering balanced mid-tier performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for balanced, CPU-intensive workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor at 2.7 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Average performance profile with 8.86 million Redis SET operations per second
Weak performance on larger LLM text generation due to memory limitations
Well-suited for software compilation, static web serving, and moderate compute tasks
